narrative: Jindal Stainless was the clear FY2025 ESG benchmark, with 61 rank-one outcomes and only 8 rank-four outcomes. The performance was led by structural environmental advantage and stronger disclosure maturity. It ranked first on Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3, physical GHG intensity and Scope 1+2 turnover intensity, with 1.85 tCO2e/tcs, reflecting stainless-steel scrap/EAF economics, renewable sourcing, biofuel replacement, waste-heat recovery, hot charging and process-efficiency initiatives. It also led absolute energy, non-renewable energy, physical energy intensity, absolute water withdrawal and consumption, turnover water intensity, particulate matter and waste-generation intensity. The FY2025 BRSR was prepared on a standalone basis and obtained reasonable assurance from SGS India, including BRSR Core, non-core indicators and GHG Scope 1, 2 and 3 disclosures. Socially, Jindal was the safety leader by a wide margin, ranking first on LTIFR, TRIFR, fatalities and affected-person indicators, and recovered to 100% training coverage across all four training metrics. Governance and stakeholder performance also improved: it led value-chain environmental assessment at 100%, tied on sustainable sourcing and had the best Board diversity at 27.28%. The weaknesses were narrower but important: female employee share and wage share remained last, total waste recovered was low due to scale and stainless mix, and trading-house dependence remained high. Overall, FY2025 shows Jindal as the operational ESG leader, though not the scale, workforce-diversity or channel-transparency benchmark.
